title: Caregiver Registration & Identity Verification - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of the Caregiver Registration & Identity Verification flow on the AT/AD platform. Covers personal and contact information form fields (First Name, Last Name, Mobile, Email), location dropdowns (State, District, Mandal), mobile OTP verification (send, expiry, retry, resend with cooldown), email verification (OTP or link, configurable), optional Aadhaar field with 12-digit validation and masking, duplicate mobile/email detection with login redirect, and mandatory field enforcement blocking registration. Linked bugs SCRUM-752 (Email OTP input disappears after Resend) and SCRUM-753 (Resend OTP button text not visible) may impact OTP test execution.

inScope: Personal information form (First Name, Last Name - mandatory, alphabets only)
Contact information (Mobile number - mandatory, 10 digits; Email ID - mandatory, valid format)
Mobile number OTP verification (send, enter, verify)
OTP expiry and retry limits enforcement
Aadhaar field (optional unless mandated by policy, 12 digits, masked, securely stored)
Duplicate mobile/email detection with 'Account exists' message and login option
Edge cases: expired OTP, max retries, special characters, boundary values
Address details (Pincode - mandatory, Complete Address - mandatory)
outOfScope: Post-registration PwD management flows (covered in SCRUM-363/372/378)
Backend API internals, encryption, and secure storage implementation
Consent and communication preferences (covered in SCRUM-357)

linkedBugs.summary: Email OTP input field disappears after clicking Resend OTP
linkedBugs.impact: May cause email OTP resend test cases to fail

linkedBugs.summary: Resend OTP button text is not visible in Email OTP section
linkedBugs.impact: May cause Resend OTP button identification to fail

testCases.title: Verify all mandatory fields are displayed on caregiver registration form
testCases.category: A. Personal & Contact Information - Form Display
testCases.preconditions: User has selected Caregiver account type from sign-up page; Registration form is loaded
steps: 1. Navigate to caregiver registration page
2. Verify First Name field is displayed with label and mandatory indicator
3. Verify Last Name field is displayed with label and mandatory indicator
4. Verify Mobile Number field is displayed with +91 country code prefix
5. Verify Email ID field is displayed with label and mandatory indicator
6. Verify State dropdown is displayed with label and mandatory indicator
7. Verify District dropdown is displayed with label and mandatory indicator
8. Verify Mandal dropdown is displayed with label and mandatory indicator
9. Verify Aadhaar Number field is displayed (optional indicator or no asterisk)
10. Verify all mandatory fields have asterisk or required indicator
expectedResults: All mandatory fields are visible: First Name, Last Name, Mobile Number, Email ID, State, District, Mandal
Aadhaar Number field is visible and marked as optional
Mobile Number field shows +91 country code prefix
All mandatory fields have clear labels and required indicators (asterisk)
Form layout is clean and fields are logically grouped
testCases.postconditions: Registration form is displayed with all fields visible

testCases.title: Verify successful caregiver registration with all valid mandatory fields
testCases.category: A. Personal & Contact Information - Happy Path
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page; OTP services are functional
steps: 1. Enter valid First Name: 'Priya' (alphabetic characters only)
2. Enter valid Last Name: 'Sharma' (alphabetic characters only)
3. Enter valid 10-digit Mobile Number: '9876543210'
4. Click 'Send OTP' for mobile and complete OTP verification
5. Enter valid Email ID: 'priya.sharma@example.com'
6. Complete email verification (OTP or link)
7. Select State from dropdown: 'Telangana'
8. Select District from dropdown: 'Hyderabad'
9. Select Mandal from dropdown: 'Secunderabad'
10. Leave Aadhaar field empty (optional)
11. Verify all fields show valid state (no errors)
12. Verify registration can proceed (submit button enabled)
13. Submit registration form
14. Verify successful registration confirmation
expectedResults: All fields accept valid input without errors
Mobile OTP verification completes successfully
Email verification completes successfully
Location dropdowns populate correctly in cascade (State → District → Mandal)
Registration proceeds successfully with confirmation message
Caregiver account is created in the system
testCases.postconditions: Caregiver account is created; User is redirected to appropriate post-registration page

testCases.title: Verify First Name and Last Name accept only alphabetic characters
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Enter numeric characters in First Name: '12345'
2. Verify inline validation error for non-alphabetic input
3. Enter special characters in First Name: '@#$%'
4. Verify inline validation error
5. Enter alphanumeric mix in First Name: 'Priya123'
6. Verify inline validation error
7. Enter valid alphabetic First Name: 'Priya'
8. Verify no validation error
9. Repeat steps 1-8 for Last Name field
10. Enter name with spaces: 'Mary Jane'
11. Verify behavior for names with spaces
12. Enter single character name: 'A'
13. Verify minimum length behavior
14. Enter very long name (50+ characters)
15. Verify maximum length behavior
expectedResults: Numeric characters trigger validation error: 'Only alphabetic characters allowed' or similar
Special characters trigger validation error
Alphanumeric mix triggers validation error
Valid alphabetic names are accepted without error
Inline validation appears immediately on invalid input
Boundary values (single char, max length) are handled gracefully
testCases.postconditions: Name field validation is verified for all input types

testCases.title: Verify Mobile Number field accepts only 10 digits with +91 prefix
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Verify +91 country code is displayed as non-editable prefix
2. Enter less than 10 digits: '98765'
3. Verify validation error for insufficient digits
4. Enter more than 10 digits: '98765432101'
5. Verify field restricts to 10 digits or shows error
6. Enter alphabetic characters: 'abcdefghij'
7. Verify field rejects non-numeric input
8. Enter special characters: '!@#$%^&*()'
9. Verify field rejects special characters
10. Enter valid 10-digit number: '9876543210'
11. Verify number is accepted without error
12. Verify Send OTP button becomes available after valid entry
expectedResults: +91 country code is displayed and non-editable
Less than 10 digits triggers validation error
More than 10 digits is either blocked or triggers error
Non-numeric characters are rejected or blocked from entry
Valid 10-digit number is accepted without error
Send OTP button is enabled after valid number entry
testCases.postconditions: Mobile number validation is verified

testCases.title: Verify Email ID format validation
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Enter invalid email without @: 'notanemail'
2. Verify inline validation error for invalid format
3. Enter email without domain: 'user@'
4. Verify validation error
5. Enter email without TLD: 'user@domain'
6. Verify validation error
7. Enter email with spaces: 'user @domain.com'
8. Verify validation error
9. Enter email with multiple @: 'user@@domain.com'
10. Verify validation error
11. Enter valid email: 'priya.sharma@example.com'
12. Verify email is accepted without error
expectedResults: All invalid email formats trigger inline validation error
Error message: 'Please enter a valid email address' or similar
Valid email format is accepted without error
Validation triggers on blur or as user types
testCases.postconditions: Email validation is verified for all input types

testCases.title: Verify Mobile OTP verification flow (send, enter, verify)
testCases.preconditions: User has entered valid 10-digit mobile number on registration form
steps: 1. Enter valid mobile number: '9876543210'
2. Click 'Send OTP' button for mobile
3. Verify OTP sent success message is displayed
4. Verify OTP input field appears
5. Enter the received OTP
6. Click 'Verify' button
7. Verify success message: 'Mobile number verified successfully' or similar
8. Verify mobile field shows verified status (checkmark or green indicator)
9. Verify mobile number field becomes read-only after verification
expectedResults: Send OTP button triggers OTP delivery to mobile
Success message confirms OTP sent
OTP input field is displayed with correct number of digit boxes
Correct OTP entry results in successful verification
Verified status indicator (checkmark/green) is shown
Mobile field becomes non-editable after verification
testCases.postconditions: Mobile number is verified; Verification status is stored

testCases.title: Verify incorrect Mobile OTP entry shows error and allows retry
testCases.preconditions: OTP has been sent to mobile number; OTP input field is visible
steps: 1. Send OTP to mobile number
2. Enter incorrect OTP: '000000'
3. Click 'Verify' button
4. Verify error message is displayed: 'Invalid OTP' or similar
5. Verify retry is allowed (input field remains active)
6. Enter incorrect OTP again
7. Verify error message and remaining retry count (if displayed)
8. Enter correct OTP
9. Verify successful verification after previous failures
expectedResults: Incorrect OTP triggers error message: 'Invalid OTP. Please try again' or similar
Retry is allowed with clear indication
Retry count or remaining attempts may be displayed
Correct OTP after failed attempts still verifies successfully
testCases.postconditions: OTP retry error handling is verified

testCases.title: Verify OTP expiry enforcement
testCases.preconditions: OTP has been sent to mobile number
steps: 1. Send OTP to mobile number
2. Observe expiry timer if displayed
3. Wait for OTP to expire (do not enter OTP within validity period)
4. Enter the expired OTP
5. Click 'Verify' button
6. Verify error message indicating OTP has expired
7. Verify user is prompted to resend OTP
expectedResults: OTP expiry timer is displayed (if applicable)
Expired OTP triggers error: 'OTP has expired. Please resend' or similar
User is prompted to request a new OTP
Expired OTP cannot be used for verification
testCases.postconditions: OTP expiry enforcement is verified

testCases.title: Verify Resend OTP with cooldown timer
testCases.preconditions: OTP has been sent to mobile number; OTP input field is visible
steps: 1. Send OTP to mobile number
2. Verify 'Resend OTP' option is displayed
3. Verify Resend OTP has a cooldown timer (e.g., 30 seconds)
4. Attempt to click Resend OTP during cooldown
5. Verify button is disabled or click is ignored during cooldown
6. Wait for cooldown to complete
7. Click 'Resend OTP'
8. Verify new OTP is sent successfully (success message)
9. Verify OTP input field remains visible
10. Enter new OTP and verify successfully
expectedResults: Resend OTP option is available after initial OTP send
Cooldown timer is displayed and prevents immediate resend
Resend OTP button is disabled during cooldown period
After cooldown, Resend OTP sends a new OTP successfully
OTP input field remains visible after resend (ref: SCRUM-752 bug)
New OTP verifies successfully
testCases.postconditions: Resend OTP with cooldown is verified

testCases.title: Verify OTP retry limits enforcement
testCases.preconditions: OTP has been sent to mobile number
steps: 1. Send OTP to mobile number
2. Enter incorrect OTP and click Verify
3. Repeat step 2 until maximum retry limit is reached
4. Verify temporary lock or block message is displayed
5. Verify OTP input and Verify button are disabled
6. Verify lock duration is communicated to user
7. Wait for lock period to expire (if applicable)
8. Verify OTP verification is re-enabled after lock period
expectedResults: After maximum retry attempts, verification is temporarily locked
Lock message clearly states duration and reason
OTP input is disabled during lock period
After lock period expires, verification is re-enabled
testCases.postconditions: OTP retry limit enforcement is verified

testCases.title: Verify Email verification flow (OTP or link, configurable)
testCases.preconditions: User has entered valid email on registration form
steps: 1. Enter valid email: 'priya.sharma@example.com'
2. Click 'Send OTP' or 'Verify Email' button
3. Verify verification method is triggered (OTP sent or verification link sent)
4. If OTP: Verify OTP input field appears
5. If OTP: Enter the received OTP and click Verify
6. If Link: Verify message indicating verification link sent to email
7. Verify success message: 'Email verified successfully' or similar
8. Verify email field shows verified status (checkmark or green indicator)
9. Verify email field becomes read-only after verification
expectedResults: Email verification is triggered via configured method (OTP or link)
Success message confirms verification method sent
Correct verification results in successful email verification
Verified status indicator is shown on email field
Email field becomes non-editable after verification
testCases.postconditions: Email is verified; Verification status is stored

testCases.title: Verify Email OTP resend does not hide input field (SCRUM-752)
testCases.preconditions: Email OTP has been sent; OTP input field is visible; Resend OTP cooldown has elapsed
steps: 1. Enter valid email and click Send OTP
2. Verify OTP input field is displayed
3. Wait for Resend OTP cooldown to complete
4. Click 'Resend OTP' for email
5. Verify OTP input field remains visible after resend
6. Verify new OTP can be entered in the input field
7. Enter new OTP and verify successfully
expectedResults: OTP input field remains visible after clicking Resend OTP
User can enter the new OTP without issues
New OTP verifies successfully
Bug SCRUM-752 regression: Input field does NOT disappear after resend
testCases.postconditions: Email OTP resend regression is verified

testCases.title: Verify Resend OTP button text is visible in Email OTP section (SCRUM-753)
testCases.preconditions: Email OTP has been sent; OTP input field is visible
steps: 1. Enter valid email and click Send OTP
2. Verify OTP input field and Resend OTP button are displayed
3. Verify Resend OTP button has visible text label: 'Resend OTP'
4. Verify button text is readable and not blank/invisible
5. Verify button is accessible (proper contrast, readable font)
expectedResults: Resend OTP button displays visible text: 'Resend OTP'
Button text is readable with proper contrast
Button is not blank or showing only background color
Bug SCRUM-753 regression: Button text IS visible
testCases.postconditions: Resend OTP button visibility regression is verified

testCases.title: Verify Aadhaar field is optional and accepts 12 digits with masking
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Verify Aadhaar field is marked as optional (no mandatory indicator)
2. Leave Aadhaar field empty
3. Fill all other mandatory fields with valid data
4. Verify registration can proceed without Aadhaar
5. Enter less than 12 digits in Aadhaar: '12345678'
6. Verify validation error for insufficient digits
7. Enter more than 12 digits: '1234567890123'
8. Verify field restricts to 12 digits or shows error
9. Enter non-numeric characters: 'abcdefghijkl'
10. Verify field rejects non-numeric input
11. Enter valid 12-digit Aadhaar: '123456789012'
12. Verify input is masked (e.g., XXXX-XXXX-9012)
13. Verify Aadhaar is accepted without error
expectedResults: Aadhaar field is optional - registration proceeds without it
Less than 12 digits triggers validation error when value is entered
More than 12 digits is blocked or triggers error
Non-numeric characters are rejected
Valid 12-digit Aadhaar is accepted
Aadhaar input is masked for privacy (only last 4 digits visible)
testCases.postconditions: Aadhaar field optional behavior and validation verified

testCases.title: Verify duplicate mobile number shows 'Account exists' with login option
testCases.preconditions: A caregiver account already exists with mobile number '9876543210'
steps: 1. Navigate to caregiver registration page
2. Enter the already-registered mobile number: '9876543210'
3. Attempt to send OTP or proceed with registration
4. Verify error message: 'Account already exists' or similar
5. Verify login/sign-in option or link is provided alongside the error
6. Click the login/sign-in link
7. Verify user is redirected to caregiver sign-in page
expectedResults: Duplicate mobile number triggers 'Account already exists' message
Login option is provided alongside the error message
Clicking login redirects to caregiver sign-in page
Registration is blocked for duplicate mobile number
testCases.postconditions: Duplicate mobile detection and login redirect verified

testCases.title: Verify duplicate email shows 'Account exists' with login option
testCases.preconditions: A caregiver account already exists with email 'existing@example.com'
steps: 1. Navigate to caregiver registration page
2. Enter the already-registered email: 'existing@example.com'
3. Attempt to verify email or proceed with registration
4. Verify error message: 'Account already exists' or similar
5. Verify login/sign-in option or link is provided alongside the error
6. Click the login/sign-in link
7. Verify user is redirected to caregiver sign-in page
expectedResults: Duplicate email triggers 'Account already exists' message
Login option is provided alongside the error message
Clicking login redirects to caregiver sign-in page
Registration is blocked for duplicate email
testCases.postconditions: Duplicate email detection and login redirect verified

testCases.title: Verify registration is blocked when mandatory fields are empty
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Leave all fields empty
2. Attempt to submit registration form
3. Verify submit button is disabled or form submission is blocked
4. Fill only First Name, leave rest empty
5. Verify inline validation errors appear for all other empty mandatory fields
6. Fill all fields except State dropdown
7. Verify State field shows required error
8. Fill all fields except District dropdown
9. Verify District field shows required error
10. Fill all fields except Mandal dropdown
11. Verify Mandal field shows required error
12. Fill all fields except Mobile Number
13. Verify Mobile Number field shows required error
14. Fill all fields except Email ID
15. Verify Email ID field shows required error
expectedResults: Submit button is disabled when mandatory fields are empty
Inline validation errors appear for each empty mandatory field
Error messages are clear and specific (e.g., 'First Name is required')
Registration cannot proceed with any mandatory field missing
No partial registration occurs
Each mandatory field (First Name, Last Name, Mobile, Email, State, District, Mandal) is individually enforced
testCases.postconditions: Form remains on registration page with validation errors displayed

testCases.title: Verify registration blocked without mobile OTP verification
testCases.preconditions: User has filled all mandatory fields with valid data; Email is verified
steps: 1. Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
2. Complete email verification
3. Do NOT verify mobile number (skip OTP)
4. Attempt to submit registration
5. Verify registration is blocked
6. Verify error or indication that mobile verification is required
expectedResults: Registration is blocked without mobile OTP verification
Clear indication that mobile verification is required
Submit button remains disabled or error is shown on attempt
testCases.postconditions: Registration blocked without mobile verification confirmed

testCases.title: Verify registration blocked without email verification
testCases.preconditions: User has filled all mandatory fields with valid data; Mobile is verified
steps: 1. Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
2. Complete mobile OTP verification
3. Do NOT verify email (skip email verification)
4. Attempt to submit registration
5. Verify registration is blocked
6. Verify error or indication that email verification is required
expectedResults: Registration is blocked without email verification
Clear indication that email verification is required
Submit button remains disabled or error is shown on attempt
testCases.postconditions: Registration blocked without email verification confirmed

testCases.title: Verify Send OTP button is disabled before valid mobile number entry
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Verify Send OTP button for mobile is disabled initially (no number entered)
2. Enter partial mobile number: '98765'
3. Verify Send OTP button remains disabled
4. Enter invalid characters: 'abcde'
5. Verify Send OTP button remains disabled
6. Enter valid 10-digit number: '9876543210'
7. Verify Send OTP button becomes enabled
expectedResults: Send OTP button is disabled when mobile field is empty
Send OTP button remains disabled for partial or invalid numbers
Send OTP button is enabled only after valid 10-digit number is entered
testCases.postconditions: Send OTP button enablement logic verified

testCases.title: Verify form retains data on validation error (no data loss)
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page with some fields filled
steps: 1. Fill First Name: 'Priya'
2. Fill Last Name: 'Sharma'
3. Fill Mobile Number: '9876543210'
4. Fill Email: 'priya.sharma@example.com'
5. Select State: 'Telangana'
6. Select District: 'Hyderabad'
7. Enter invalid Aadhaar: '12345' (less than 12 digits)
8. Trigger validation (blur or submit attempt)
9. Verify Aadhaar validation error is shown
10. Verify all previously entered data is retained (First Name, Last Name, Mobile, Email, State, District)
11. Correct the Aadhaar field
12. Verify all data is still intact
expectedResults: Validation error is shown for invalid Aadhaar
All previously entered valid data is retained in form fields
No data loss occurs when validation errors are triggered
Correcting the error allows form to proceed without re-entering other fields
testCases.postconditions: Form data retention on validation error verified

testCases.title: Verify mobile number starting with 0 or invalid prefix
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Enter mobile number starting with 0: '0987654321'
2. Verify validation behavior (Indian mobile numbers start with 6-9)
3. Enter mobile number starting with 1: '1234567890'
4. Verify validation behavior
5. Enter mobile number starting with 5: '5678901234'
6. Verify validation behavior
7. Enter valid mobile starting with 9: '9876543210'
8. Verify accepted without error
expectedResults: Mobile numbers not starting with 6-9 may trigger validation error (Indian format)
Valid Indian mobile numbers (starting 6-9) are accepted
Validation enforces Indian mobile number format if applicable
testCases.postconditions: Mobile number prefix validation verified

testCases.title: Verify consent checkbox is mandatory for registration
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Navigate to caregiver registration page
2. Verify consent checkbox is visible
3. Verify consent is unchecked by default
4. Verify Complete Registration button is disabled without consent
expectedResults: Consent checkbox is visible on registration form
Consent checkbox is unchecked by default
Complete Registration button is disabled without consent checked
testCases.postconditions: Consent checkbox mandatory behavior verified

testCases.title: Verify Pincode field accepts only valid 6-digit input
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Verify Pincode field is visible
2. Enter valid 6-digit pincode
3. Verify pincode is accepted
expectedResults: Pincode field is visible on registration form
Valid 6-digit pincode is accepted without error
testCases.postconditions: Pincode validation verified

testCases.title: Verify Aadhaar privacy information is displayed
testCases.preconditions: User is on caregiver registration page
steps: 1. Navigate to caregiver registration page
2. Verify Aadhaar field is visible
3. Verify Aadhaar privacy and usage information text is displayed
expectedResults: Aadhaar field is visible
Privacy and usage information is displayed near the Aadhaar field
